text: Ka Farisii dima la daa boosiya, ni, “Ka di niŋi la wula ka Musa daa zaali zaaligu la, ni, sɔ nin toaai n‑za'asi u pɔ'a? - ni, u zaŋŋma pɔ'aza'asigu gbaŋŋu n‑ti u pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ ka u bakki.”
translated: And the Pharisees came and asked him, "If it is according to the law that Moses gave, why do you send away your wife? - their question was to test him, to see what he would say."

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ka doo maa daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “Zaaligu bɔ n nyɛ a?” Ka Yisa daa yɛli, “Ni, ‘Di ku ninsaala.’, ‘Di tum zina.’, ‘Di zu.’, ‘Di di ziri sɛhara n‑lusi i kpeaa u yɛlli ni.’,
translated: And they asked him saying, "Teacher, what shall we do?" And Jesus answered, "Do not collect more than what is required.", "Do not take bribes.", "Do not steal.", "Do not bear false witness against your neighbor."

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Amaa tuntumniba maa ŋɔn daa nya puu maa daana bii la, ba daa yɛli n‑ti taaba, ni, ‘Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u, ka mari u faari maa.’
translated: But the farmers who saw the fruit on the tree were saying to themselves, saying, 'This is the fruit of our tree. Come let us kill him, and take his fruit.'

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ammi, ya na bu karim Sɔppu la? - ni#:- ‘Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu; Ti Dugma tuma maa nla, Ka di nyɛ la alahazibu n‑ti ti.’ - ya bu baŋŋi lala sɔppu maa?”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "Truly, do you not understand the Scripture? - that:- 'The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone, This is the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes; It is our God's work, And it is wonderful to us.' - do you not know this scripture at all?"

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: U daa tum u daaba ni ba kyaŋŋi nti booli ŋɔn pun zuusi seeba maa, ni, ba kyɛm na. Amaa ba daa bu sakki n‑kyɛ na.
translated: They used to build their houses with mud walls and thatch roofs, and they would plaster them. But they were not as strong as now.

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: Amaa Naa maa ŋɔn daa kpɛ, ni u ti nya ban daa la'am na la, u daa nya dɔsɔ ka u bu yɛ pɔ'adiri kyuu neema.
translated: But God is not like that, if you do not see him with your eyes, you see evidence that he exists in many things.

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Tɔ; ka lala saha maa ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa.
translated: So; when the time came for the Sadducees - who do not believe that the dead will rise - they came and asked him a question.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Ya gɔsima! Ya Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa yi kpalim la daboo.
translated: Oh my goodness! Your God has given you great wisdom.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Di laa m‑boonni sɔ ya Ba, duniya wa puuni: ya Ba yinni m bɛ ni n‑ti ya, ka ŋɔn mun bɛ la saazugu.
translated: They were my children and you, Father, the world hated them: you, Father, gave them to me so that they may not perish, but have eternal life.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Yan di laa n‑kyɛ ka ba boonni ya ba toondaandima: ya toondaana bɛ ni n‑ti ya, ka ŋɔn mun n nyɛ Masiiya maa.
translated: They went and told their fellow disciples: we have found the Messiah (which means Christ).

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya ŋmanni la hoo gbala ka ba zaŋŋi tampeelli n‑ta a ka a veela, amaa a puuni na paali ninvugkpiimma kɔba ni da'aseesi an kyisiri zaa.
translated: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya maanni la gbala n‑tiri anabidima, ka kur kyɛ ka ninvugsuma dabɔya veela,
translated: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of the bones of the dead and everything unclean.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Di saha maa ka niriba pam nin ti birisi ka labi nyaaŋŋa, ka seeba mun figsiri taaba ka yaai taaba.
translated: They will also help people with their work and give good advice, and teach some things to children and adults.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: Ni, “Amaa di daari maa, bee di saha, ŋɔn ya m‑paai sahanseaa la ka sɔsɔ bu mi deede, halli malaakadima ban bɛ arizana ni la bu mi, Naawunni Bii maa gba bu mi, na'ala ti Ba maa kɔkɔ m mi.
translated: And, "Even if I die, or suffer, no one can separate me from God's love, not even the angels or any power, not God's Son will abandon me, because of their great love for me.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Dinŋɔ zugu ya gba niŋma siri, dama sahanseaa yan bu teesiri u yɛla maa, lala saha ka Ninsaala Bii maa ti ya kyɛ na.”
translated: "The world has become very difficult, because the rains have not fallen on their time, especially the rain that God's Son said would come."

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Yɛlimaŋni ka n siri yɛtti ya, ni, u nin ti zaŋŋi u bunseelli kam fukumsi n‑niŋi lala daabri nuu ni.
translated: If you do not want to do that, then, they will put you in their prison or fine you a lot of money.

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: Di nyaaŋŋa ka daaba wa dugma la daa kuli na, ka yɛli, ni, ba niŋma ligri maa laasaabu ka u nya.
translated: The women and men who are in the house are working, and they say that their children should go to school so they can learn.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Ka ba nin la'asi duniya wa niriba zaa u saani na. Ŋɔn mun ti ya wɔligi ba taaba, hoo bunkɔbgura ŋɔn wɔligri u bunkɔbri seem, nti kyɛ ka peesi m bɛ kpa, buusi mun bɛ la kpa.
translated: And they will not leave the world to the children as they found it. Whatever we do to them, or the example we set for our children, if it is not good, the thing that will come will not be good.

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Dama kɔm ŋɔn daa mari ma sahanseaa la, ya daa bu zaŋŋi diibu n‑diisi ma; kɔnyuuri gba ŋɔn daa mari ma, ya daa bu zaŋŋi koom n‑ti ma ni n nyu:
translated: The one who had received five talents came and brought five more talents, saying, 'Master, you gave me five talents; look, I have gained five more talents.'

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Ka u daa labi u nyaandɔliba maa saani na n‑yɛli ba, “Ya na kpalim n‑gbisiri ni ka vuusira?”. Ni, “Saha maa paaya. Ya nyama, ba pun yi n nyaaŋŋa ka figsi man Ninsaala Biiya n‑niŋi ala'akyi dima nuu ni.
translated: And when they asked their leaders about this matter, saying, "Why do you trouble us and oppress us?". Then, "It is not our fault. You know, they have given us orders to complete God's work within three days."

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: U daa labi nyaandɔliba la saani na ka nya, ka babata maa zaa daa gbisiri ni; ka u booli Piita, ni, “Saimoon, ni wula? I naa n‑ku toaai n‑gali goom halli hawa yinni n‑ti ma?”
translated: They had left the disciples there and went away, and their father was also in the boat with them; and they called Peter, saying, "Simon, are you asleep? Could you not stay awake for one hour?"

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: Ŋɔn daa na kur tɔ'asiri maa, nnya Gyuudasi ŋɔn paari na, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi la ni yinni. Niriba pam daa dɔli u: ba daa yi la gyeeŋŋu kpambaya ni Izraɛli kpamma saani na, ka ba daa mari ba takoobidima ni dɔya na.
translated: When they heard these things, the Jews who were there became very angry and gnashed their teeth at him. Everyone was watching him: they saw his face shining like an angel's face and like the face of Moses when he came down from Mount Sinai with the two stone tablets.

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Ka ban daa dɔli Yisa maa sɔ daa foaai u takoobi n‑kyɛ kpambaya maa bisɔ n‑fikki u tubri m‑basi.
translated: They did not believe in Jesus so they could not understand his teaching and follow his way of truth.

ref: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
text: ka boosiri u, ni, “Nyin Masiiya, nyin anabi wa, bugma m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn ŋmɛ i wa!”
translated: saying to him, "You are the Messiah, you are the prophet we have been waiting for!"

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni kpambaya maa ŋɔn daa galimni u la, u daa bu yɛli seelli.
translated: And when the children were playing with the ball and it got lost, they all cried together.

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Ka daa zaŋŋi goosi m‑paam m‑pili u zugu hoo naam zupiliga, ka zaŋŋi kika'ari n‑gbibli u nudirigu hoo naam damni; ka daa gbaantiri u tooni m‑maanni u ansarisi, ni, “Ndana, m Ba Izraɛli Naaba!”
translated: And they put a purple robe on him and placed a crown of thorns on his head and saluted him; and they bowed before him and mocked him saying, "Hail, King of the Jews!"

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ka ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑sa. Di saha ka ba daa puri u sutura n‑tɔri taaba, n‑toori tɛtɛ ni ba ti nya a puribu soori.
translated: If they had not put him in the prison house, he would have escaped. Because if they had put him in the chief's room, he would have easily seen his friends and they would have helped him escape.

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa zinni bɛni ka gɔsiri u ni.
translated: The children did not know how to respect him.

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Ka Pailiti daa yɛli ba, “Ya mari la ya maŋtoori tɔbbiisi; tɔ - kur kyɛma n‑niŋi yan nin nyaŋŋi seem n‑guri ka suŋŋasuŋŋa.”
translated: And Pilate said to them, "You have your own guards; go and guard it as well as you can."

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: Ya wulisima ba, ni, ba dɔla man zaali ya noori seem maa zaa. Ka ya baŋŋma, ni, n nin bɛ ya saani, halli ni duniya wa saha maa naari.”
translated: They will not be able to do anything to you at all. And in the end, I will be with you, until the world comes to an end."

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Ka tɔbbiisi maa daa deaai ba ligri ka daa tum ban daa bulisi ba seem la. Di zugu lahabaari kanŋɔ na bɛ Yɛhudiya dima maa saani halli ni zuna.
translated: They did not want to let them go and did not allow them to worship their God. This news reached all of Judea within a few days.

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Ka di saha maa ka u kɔmni maa daa basi u ka u daa niŋi kasi.
translated: If you do not repent and turn away from your sins, you will perish.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Ka Yisa daa kpaamni u ni nimmoaa n‑kyeoosi u:
translated: And Jesus called him and said to him:

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Amaa doo maa daa yi n‑kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa wum lahabaari maa, halli ka di ti niŋi ka Yisa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa kam puuni ka ba nya u, amaa u daa kpalim m‑bɛ la moo ni. Amaa di zaa yɔri, niriba daa yiiri la lugri kam n‑kyɛnni u saani na.
translated: But even though they did not believe all the news about the resurrection, because they knew that Jesus had died on the cross and they had seen him, but he had risen from death. But despite all this, people continued to doubt until they saw him themselves.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli doo maa, “Doaam n‑zaani sunsukkuni!”,
translated: They did not know that Jesus had said, "Lazarus is dead!"

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: ni Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii bii la, n daa bɛ ni, ni u sunzɔ Gyɔni - ban ka u daa pa ba yupaa, ni ‘Bɔ'anɛrigyeesi’ ,
translated: and James, Zebedee's son, and his brother John - whom he named, and 'Boanerges',

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Ka Yisa ma, ni u sunzɔppa, daa paai na n‑zaani samanni ka tum sɔ ni u kpɛ m‑booli u na.
translated: And Jesus, with his disciples, went out to the villages of Caesarea to ask his disciples.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Tɔ, di saha maa ka u bumburiseesi daa lu kuga ziiya ka tanni ka ni see beela fiiŋ, ka daa tɔ bula yooma dama tanni maa daa bu zoaai;
translated: So, we thank God that our elders have left us with good things to follow and see a good path, that they did not leave us in darkness or leave us to wander aimlessly;

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Tɔ, ya wumma: ŋɔn butti kyi la n nyɛ hoo ŋɔn moonni Naawunni yɛligu.
translated: So, you see: what we have done here is that we have explained God's word.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Ay! - Tanni maa daa tumni ka maŋtoori tuma n‑kyɛ ka kyi maa ti niŋi: a daa daŋŋi m‑buli m‑pusi n‑dɔ'ai, halli nti piri.
translated: Ah! - Therefore you should not think that the kingdom of heaven is like what I am telling you now: it is like a mustard seed, the smallest of all seeds.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Ka ka daa buli, n‑zoaai n‑niŋi buntitaari n‑gaari zeeri bumbula zaasa, ka daa tɔ wula, halli ka niinsi daa tɛ ba teeri a zugu ka nyari ka vaari maasim.”
translated: If they do not agree, my children and grandchildren will continue to fight until they die, even if it takes years, until people see their heads and know that they have truly suffered.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Halli ba daa pun zaŋŋi kutti m‑ba u nuusi ni u nɔba, amaa u daa kur kyisiri a ni - sɔsɔ daa ka yaa n nin toaai n‑gbaai u suŋŋa.
translated: They did not even have time to eat and drink, but they had to hurry - because it was not long before they reached their destination.

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
text: Ka u daa ti ba soori, ka ba daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni - kurikyuudima maa mun daa paai tusa ayi - ka ba daa vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni ka koom daa di ba.
translated: And they would go to their farms, and they would go and gather and cut firewood - the firewood that they would use to cook food - and they would fetch water to put in the pot so that food would be for them.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Ka u daa pɔ noori ka yɛli u, “N nin ti i nyin boori seelli kam zaa halli di yi nyɛ n naam tingbaŋŋu maa purigri.”
translated: And they answered and said to him, "We do not have five loaves of bread except these that we have in our hands."

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ka ba daa doaai n‑kpɛ ba nyariŋŋu la n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ba kɔkɔa.
translated: If they did not take care of their children and give them good advice, they would be lost.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu ni u ti puusi Naawunni.
translated: And when they saw that they were not succeeding, they decided to go back and they stopped worshipping God.

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Anabi Aizaaya daa pun mari yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn daa bugi n‑yɛli ya birikɔ'anu dima yɛla seem la; ka daa sɔbi, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- ‘Niriba wa gyeemni ma la ni ba nangbana. Ba sufu mun yi n saani Ka kpalim n‑wɔ'a;
translated: And Jesus said to them, "The prophet Isaiah spoke well about you hypocrites as it is written; he said, 'The Lord says:- 'These people honor me with their lips. But their hearts are far from my presence;'

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Sɔkanŋɔ n nyɛ wulisiseesi yan mari n‑wulisiri seeba n‑kpiisiri Naawunni zaaligu. Ka yɛla pam anŋɔ taabu bɛ ni ka ya niŋna.”
translated: The reason is that the authorities and the elders are working together to develop the town. If you do this, it will bring trouble and you will be arrested.

ref: The food that you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Seelli ka ni din nin kpɛ ninsaala puuni ka kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira puuni na n kyɛ ka u leebri da'ari daana.”
translated: The world and its inhabitants are in God's hands and he does whatever he wants with them. But some people's hearts are hardened and they refuse to obey God.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: - Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa yiiri la ninsaala puuni na n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”
translated: The reason for all this trouble is that people refuse to accept that their days are numbered.
